summary: Mutual indemnification covering breaches, negligence, and third-party product claims, conditional on prompt notice.
text: Each party ("Indemnifying Party") shall defend, indemnify, and hold harmless the other party and its officers, directors, employees, agents, and successors from and against any and all claims, damages, losses, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of or relating to: (a) the Indemnifying Party's breach of any representation, warranty, or obligation under this Agreement; (b) the Indemnifying Party's negligence or willful misconduct; or (c) any third-party claim arising from the Indemnifying Party's products or services. The Indemnifying Party's obligations are conditioned upon the Indemnified Party providing prompt written notice of the claim and reasonable cooperation.

summary: Mutual waiver of indirect damages and a liability cap based on recent fees paid or a fixed dollar amount.
text: IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE TO THE OTHER FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, PUNITIVE, OR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT, REGARDLESS OF THE THEORY OF LIABILITY AND EVEN IF SUCH PARTY HAS BEEN ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES. EACH PARTY'S TOTAL CUMULATIVE LIABILITY ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT SHALL NOT EXCEED THE GREATER OF (A) THE AMOUNTS PAID BY CLIENT TO COMPANY IN THE {{lookback_months}} MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM, OR (B) {{liability_cap_amount}}.

summary: Standard mutual NDA clause with a survival period for obligations after termination.
text: Each party agrees to maintain in strict confidence all Confidential Information of the other party and to use such Confidential Information solely for the purposes of this Agreement. "Confidential Information" means any non-public information disclosed by one party to the other that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential. These obligations survive termination for {{survival_years}} years. Each party may disclose Confidential Information to its employees and contractors on a need-to-know basis, provided such persons are bound by obligations no less restrictive than those herein.

summary: Client owns all new work product; Contractor retains pre-existing IP but grants a license to the Client.
text: All work product, deliverables, inventions, developments, and intellectual property created by Contractor in connection with this Agreement ("Work Product") shall be deemed works made for hire and shall be the exclusive property of Client. To the extent any Work Product does not qualify as works made for hire, Contractor hereby assigns to Client all right, title, and interest, including all intellectual property rights, in and to such Work Product. Contractor retains ownership of any pre-existing intellectual property ("Background IP") and grants Client a non-exclusive, perpetual, royalty-free license to use Background IP incorporated into the Work Product.

summary: Allows either party to terminate the agreement without cause by providing written notice.
text: Either party may terminate this Agreement for any reason or no reason upon {{notice_period_days}} days' prior written notice to the other party. Upon termination, Client shall pay Company for all services performed and expenses incurred prior to the effective date of termination. Company shall deliver to Client all Work Product completed as of the termination date. Each party's obligations that, by their nature, should survive termination (including confidentiality, indemnification, and limitation of liability) shall survive.

summary: Permits immediate termination for material breach (with a cure period), insolvency, or ceasing operations.
text: Either party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice if the other party: (a) materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within {{cure_period_days}} days after receiving written notice thereof; (b) becomes insolvent or makes an assignment for the benefit of creditors; (c) has a receiver or administrator appointed; or (d) ceases to conduct business in the ordinary course. Termination for cause does not limit any other rights or remedies the terminating party may have.

summary: Specifies governing jurisdiction, mandates good faith negotiation, and requires binding arbitration for unresolved disputes.
text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of {{governing_state}}, without regard to its conflict of laws principles. Any dispute, claim, or controversy arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall first be subject to good faith negotiation for {{negotiation_days}} days. If unresolved, disputes shall be submitted to binding arbitration under the {{arbitration_rules}}, with a single arbitrator, conducted in English, with the seat of arbitration in {{arbitration_city_state}}. The prevailing party shall be entitled to recover its reasonable attorneys' fees.

summary: Mutual restriction on poaching employees during the agreement and for a set period after.
text: During the term of this Agreement and for {{restriction_months}} months thereafter, neither party shall directly solicit or induce any employee of the other party with whom such party had material contact during the performance of this Agreement to terminate their employment. This restriction does not apply to general solicitations through public job postings or advertisements not specifically targeting the other party's employees.

summary: Defines invoice payment windows, late payment interest rates, and conditions for service suspension.
text: Client shall pay all undisputed invoices within {{payment_window_days}} days of receipt. Late payments shall accrue interest at the lesser of {{interest_rate_percent}}% per month or the maximum rate permitted by law. Company reserves the right to suspend services upon {{suspension_notice_days}} days' written notice if invoices remain unpaid beyond {{delinquency_days}} days. Client shall reimburse Company for all pre-approved, reasonable expenses incurred in connection with the services, provided Company submits expense reports with supporting documentation.

summary: General authority and compliance warranties, a professional services warranty, and a disclaimer of implied warranties.
text: Each party represents and warrants that: (a) it has full authority to enter into and perform this Agreement; (b) this Agreement does not conflict with any other agreement to which it is a party; and (c) it will comply with all applicable laws and regulations in connection with this Agreement. Company further warrants that services will be performed in a professional and workmanlike manner consistent with industry standards. EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY SET FORTH HEREIN, EACH PARTY DISCLAIMS ALL OTHER WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING THE IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.

summary: Identifies the contracting parties with full legal names, formation states, and addresses, and establishes the effective date of the agreement.
text: This Agreement is entered into as of the date last signed below ("Effective Date"), by and between {{client_legal_name}}, a {{client_formation_state}} {{client_entity_type}} with its principal place of business at {{client_address}} ("Client"), and {{company_legal_name}}, a {{company_formation_state}} {{company_entity_type}} with its principal place of business at {{company_address}} ("Company"). Client and Company may be referred to individually as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties." No Party shall be bound by this Agreement until it has been duly executed by an authorized representative of each Party.

summary: Background recitals establishing the purpose and context of the agreement between the parties.
text: WHEREAS, Client desires to obtain certain {{services_description}} services from Company; and WHEREAS, Company desires to provide such services to Client on the terms and conditions set forth herein; NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements set forth herein, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the parties agree as follows:

summary: Defines key terms used throughout the agreement to ensure consistent interpretation.
text: As used in this Agreement, the following terms shall have the meanings set forth below. "Affiliate" means any entity that directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with a party. "Agreement" means this agreement together with all exhibits, schedules, and statements of work attached hereto. "Applicable Law" means all laws, regulations, rules, and orders applicable to a party's performance under this Agreement. "Business Day" means any day other than a Saturday, Sunday, or a day on which banks are authorized or required to be closed in the State of {{governing_state}}. "Confidential Information" has the meaning set forth in the Confidentiality section. "Effective Date" has the meaning set forth in the preamble. "Services" means the services described in each Statement of Work. "Statement of Work" or "SOW" means a written statement of work executed by both parties that describes specific services, deliverables, timelines, and fees.

summary: Excuses performance delays caused by events outside a party's reasonable control, with notice and mitigation obligations.
text: Neither party shall be liable for any delay or failure to perform its obligations under this Agreement to the extent such delay or failure is caused by circumstances beyond such party's reasonable control, including but not limited to acts of God, natural disasters, pandemics, epidemics, war, terrorism, riots, embargoes, acts of civil or military authority, fire, floods, accidents, strikes, or failures of third-party telecommunications or internet service providers (each, a "Force Majeure Event"). The party affected by a Force Majeure Event shall: (a) provide prompt written notice to the other party describing the Force Majeure Event and its expected duration; (b) use commercially reasonable efforts to mitigate the effects of and overcome the Force Majeure Event; and (c) resume performance as soon as reasonably practicable. If a Force Majeure Event continues for more than {{force_majeure_threshold_days}} consecutive days, either party may terminate this Agreement upon written notice without liability, provided that Client shall remain obligated to pay for all services performed prior to the Force Majeure Event.

summary: Requires written agreement for amendments and clarifies that failure to enforce a right does not constitute waiver of that right.
text: This Agreement may not be amended, modified, or supplemented except by a written instrument signed by authorized representatives of both parties. No failure or delay by either party in exercising any right, power, or privilege under this Agreement shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise of any right, power, or privilege preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any other right, power, or privilege. No waiver of any breach or default shall be deemed a waiver of any subsequent breach or default of the same or similar nature.

summary: Specifies how formal notices must be delivered between parties and when they are deemed received.
text: All notices, requests, demands, and other communications required or permitted under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be deemed duly given: (a) when delivered personally to the recipient; (b) one (1) Business Day after deposit with a nationally recognized overnight courier service, addressed to the recipient; (c) three (3) Business Days after mailing by certified or registered mail, return receipt requested, postage prepaid, addressed to the recipient; or (d) upon transmission by email with written confirmation of receipt by the recipient (not including auto-reply). Notices to Client shall be sent to {{client_notice_address}} (Attention: {{client_notice_contact}}). Notices to Company shall be sent to {{company_notice_address}} (Attention: {{company_notice_contact}}). Either party may change its notice address by providing written notice to the other party in accordance with this section.

summary: States the agreement is the complete understanding between parties and that invalid provisions are severed without voiding the whole agreement.
text: This Agreement, together with all Statements of Work and exhibits attached hereto, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to its subject matter and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements, representations, warranties, and understandings, whether written or oral, relating to such subject matter. If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable in any jurisdiction, such invalidity, illegality, or unenforceability shall not affect any other provision of this Agreement or the validity, legality, or enforceability of such provision in any other jurisdiction, and the parties shall negotiate in good faith to replace the invalid provision with a valid provision that, to the greatest extent possible, achieves the original intent and economic effect of the invalid provision.

summary: Restricts assignment of the agreement without prior written consent, with an exception for mergers and acquisitions.
text: Neither party may assign, transfer, delegate, or subcontract any of its rights or obligations under this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other party, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld, conditioned, or delayed. Notwithstanding the foregoing, either party may assign this Agreement without consent in connection with a merger, acquisition, corporate reorganization, or sale of all or substantially all of its assets to which this Agreement relates, provided that: (a) the assignee assumes in writing all of the assigning party's obligations under this Agreement; (b) the assigning party provides the other party with prompt written notice of such assignment; and (c) the assignee is not a direct competitor of the non-assigning party. Any purported assignment in violation of this section shall be null and void. This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties and their respective permitted successors and assigns.
